WITCHER.Roll.Fumble: Fumble! (natural 1 — roll on the fumble table)
WITCHER.Roll.FumbleWarning: Fumble! Natural 1 — resolve it on the fumble table.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteNormal: Normal strike: one attack at no penalty — your standard swing.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteStrong: Strong strike: -3 to hit, but doubles the rolled damage (applied before the target's armour).
WITCHER.Attack.NoteFast: Fast strike: two attacks this action at no penalty, but each rolls damage separately.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteCharge: Charge: full round — move up to Run speed, then a strong strike at -3. If blocked, roll Physique vs the target's Physique to knock it prone.
WITCHER.Attack.NotePommel: Pommel strike: half damage, dealt as non-lethal.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteDisarm: Disarm: no damage. On a hit, the target's weapon is knocked 1d6 m away in a random direction (Scatter).
WITCHER.Attack.NoteTrip: Trip: no damage. On a hit, the target is knocked prone.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteFeint: Feint: roll Deceit instead of your first Fast strike. If the target fails an Awareness check vs your Deceit, your next attack is at +3.
WITCHER.Attack.NoteJoint: Joint attack: two attacks, both at -3 (main + off-hand weapon). The target must dodge/reposition or have two weapons (or weapon + shield) to block/parry both.
WITCHER.Brawl.StatusManual: On a hit, apply {status} (no target selected).
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteBlock: Block a melee attack with your limbs — a Brawling defense roll. A defensive reaction (no action needed), so it stays available even with no actions left. Only blocks attacks you could plausibly stop bare-handed.
WITCHER.Brawl.NotePunch: Punch: REF + Brawling to hit, dealing your Punch damage as non-lethal (bludgeoning). May be a strong or fast strike.
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteKick: Kick: REF + Brawling to hit, dealing your Kick damage as non-lethal (bludgeoning). May be a strong or fast strike.
WITCHER.Brawl.NotePushKick: Instead of dealing full damage you may push the target back a number of metres equal to your Body/3. This attack does half damage and always hits the torso.
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteCharge: Full round: move up to your Run speed, then make a strong strike (-3 to hit). If the attack is blocked, make a Physique check vs the target's Physique to knock them prone.
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteDisarm: Roll Brawling vs the target's Dodge/Escape. Either knock their weapon away (1d6/2 m in a random direction) or grab it with a free hand (-3 to the roll).
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteGrapple: Roll Brawling vs the target's Dodge/Escape. While grappled they cannot move away and take -2 to all physical actions. Prerequisite for pins, chokes and throws; each turn the target may roll Dodge/Escape vs your Brawling to slip loose.
WITCHER.Brawl.NotePin: Requires a grapple. On a success the target is immobilized and cannot move or act until they escape (Dodge/Escape vs your Brawling).
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteChoke: Requires a grapple. On a success the target is suffocating until they are able to escape (Dodge/Escape vs your Brawling).
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteThrow: Requires a grapple. The target is thrown to the ground (prone), takes damage equal to your Punch damage, and must make a Stun save at -1.
WITCHER.Brawl.NoteTrip: Kick the target's legs out from under them. On a success the target falls prone.
WITCHER.Attack.AimAutoHint: Auto-applied from the Aim status; cleared when you fire.
WITCHER.Effect.ActionAlcoholRollAdvantag: Bearer rolls Endurance twice and keeps the best when drinking alcohol. Hook this onto the Witcher race, a profession perk, or any other AE source.
WITCHER.Effect.ActionClearHangoverHint: Applying this effect immediately wipes every hangover the bearer is suffering. The source effect itself is left in place — let its duration or removal handle cleanup. Author it on a hair-of-the-dog tonic, a restorative meal, a cleric's invocation, etc.
WITCHER.Cast.DefenseNone: No defense — the effect lands automatically.
WITCHER.Cast.OverExert: Over-exertion! This cast drives {over} STA past Vigor {vigor} ({spent} cast this round) — take {dmg} damage and an elemental fumble (Core p.166).
WITCHER.Cast.OverExertWarn: Over-exertion: this cast drives {over} STA past Vigor {vigor} ({spent} this round) — you'll take {dmg} damage and an elemental fumble.
WITCHER.Cast.SignCap: A sign caps at 7 STA per cast (Core p.115).
WITCHER.Cast.DurationApplied: Active for {dur} — expires on its own.
WITCHER.Cast.NoStamina: Not enough Stamina to cast this.
WITCHER.Monster.Threat.Label: Threat
WITCHER.Monster.Complexity.Label: Complexity
WITCHER.Monster.SaddlebagsHint: Drag items or containers here to stow them on this mount. They also appear in the rider's saddlebag panel.
WITCHER.Monster.SaddlebagsEmpty: Nothing stowed — drag items or containers here.
WITCHER.Monster.StatusReactionsHint: Click a status: once for Resistant, again for Immune, again to clear.
WITCHER.Monster.SkillsHint: Only trained skills are listed. Pick one above to train it, or set a rank to 0 to drop it from the list.
WITCHER.Monster.NoSkillsYet: No trained skills yet — add one above.
WITCHER.Monster.MutagenHint: Drag a mutagen item here to link this creature's harvestable mutagen.
WITCHER.Monster.RandomLootHint: Drag items or roll tables here — the harvest picks one at random. Duplicates don't stack.
WITCHER.Monster.RandomRollsHint: How many times to roll from this pool (accepts a dice code, e.g. 1d6).
WITCHER.Monster.AttackSkillHint: Combat skill used for the to-hit roll
WITCHER.Monster.IsMount: This creature can be ridden as a mount
WITCHER.Merchant.NoCharacter: No character assigned. Assign one in your user config, or select a token, to trade.
WITCHER.Merchant.NoGM: No GM is online to handle the transaction.
WITCHER.Merchant.NoGMReply: No reply from the GM — they may be busy or disconnected. Try again.
WITCHER.Merchant.ShopEmpty: This merchant has nothing in stock.
WITCHER.Merchant.PriceRevealed: You size up its fair worth.
WITCHER.Merchant.PriceHidden: You can't tell what it's really worth.
WITCHER.Merchant.AppraisalLocked: You misjudged the goods — this merchant won't let you appraise again until tomorrow.
WITCHER.Merchant.AppraisalLockedHint: Appraisal locked until the next day — your last Business check failed.
WITCHER.Merchant.RevealTooltip: Business check to reveal fair price
WITCHER.Merchant.FairTooltip: Fair market value (revealed by Business check)
WITCHER.Merchant.SellBlurb: Offer items from your inventory. The merchant pays half base price by default; you can change the asking price before offering, and the merchant can counter.
WITCHER.Merchant.SellNeedChar: Assign a character to your user to sell items.
WITCHER.Merchant.SellIntro: Offer {item} to {merchant}. Name your price per unit.
WITCHER.Merchant.SellHint: A fair asking price is about {price} {denom} each.
WITCHER.Merchant.WalkedAway: You walk away from the deal.
WITCHER.Merchant.NegExpired: Negotiation expired — the merchant lost the thread of the deal. Make a fresh offer.
WITCHER.Merchant.ClearConfirm: This will clear the current {count} item(s) and restock from scratch. Proceed?
WITCHER.Merchant.NoPreviousStock: No previous stock to restock from.
WITCHER.Merchant.ErrEmptyPool: The saved item pool is empty.
WITCHER.Merchant.ErrNoItems: No items matched the stocking filters.
WITCHER.Merchant.PoolSaved: Saved {count} item(s) as the restock pool.
WITCHER.Merchant.GMOnlyCard: Only the GM can place merchant cards.
WITCHER.Merchant.RemoveCardConfirm: Remove this merchant card from the scene?
WITCHER.Effect.ChangesHint: Each line changes one thing about the bearer. Pick what to affect, how, and by how much.
WITCHER.Effect.EffectsHint: Each line is one action this effect performs. Pick an action, then fill in its details. Add as many as you like.
WITCHER.Effect.DurationFormulaHint: Optional dice code rolled fresh each time this is consumed (e.g. 1d6/2, 2d10) — overrides the fixed value above and uses the units selected. Rounds down, minimum 1.
WITCHER.Effect.CmQuickWithShield: Use quick items with a shield (on/off)
WITCHER.Effect.CmFreeShieldEquip: Free action to equip a shield (on/off)
WITCHER.Effect.ActionPurgeHint: When this effect lands, it ends every other toxicity-bearing potion/decoction effect on the holder (their toxicity is reclaimed with them). This is how White Honey works.
WITCHER.Effect.TempHpHint: Temporary HP — a non-regenerable buffer drained before real HP. Not raised by healing.
WITCHER.Effect.AmountHint: Flat number or roll, e.g. 2 or 1d6. Rolls are re-rolled each turn for a recurring (Each turn) modify.
WITCHER.Effect.TransferConsumableWarning: If this effect lives on a Consumable item, leave this OFF. Consumable effects are applied when a dose is used, not while the item is merely carried — keeping it on would let the buff apply for free.
WITCHER.Effect.WhenRoundStart: At the start of each round
WITCHER.Effect.WhenUndamaged: Each round no damage was taken
WITCHER.Effect.TargetHint: Type or pick a data path, e.g. system.stats.will.value
WITCHER.Effect.ConditionHint: Expression, e.g. @system.stats.toxicity.value > @system.stats.toxicity.max * 125%. Use @path, + - * /, > < >= <= == !=, and / or, and percents (25%).
WITCHER.Effect.FireCapHint: Cap on how many times this fires per effect (blank = unlimited).
WITCHER.Effect.LogicHint: Make this effect do something at the start of the bearer's turn each combat round.
WITCHER.Effect.TickHealHint: Flat number or roll, e.g. 3 or 1d6+2. Restores HP each turn.
WITCHER.Effect.TickDamageHint: Flat number or roll, e.g. 5 or 2d6. Deals HP damage each turn.
WITCHER.Effect.TickLocationHint: Toggle one or more. Damage is dealt to each selected location. "Every location" hits all six.
WITCHER.Effect.TickThroughArmorHint: When on, tick damage skips the location's armor SP.
WITCHER.Effect.TickAblateArmorHint: Erode this many Stopping Power off the armor at each struck location, every tick (fire/acid). 0 = none.
WITCHER.Effect.SuppressHint: While this effect is active, switch off RAW state penalties on the bearer.
WITCHER.Effect.SuppressDeath: Ignore death-state penalty (stats not reduced to 1/3)
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.bookSystem.Hin: Enables the 3-type book system (monster lore, skill, stress) on valuable items. Disable for RAW Witcher TRPG.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.stress.Name: WIP — Homebrew: Stress Mechanic
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.stress.Hint: Adds a stress counter to characters, with stress books to release it.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.foodAndDrink.H: Enables food/drink charges and the drunkenness track (Sober Up). Disable for RAW Witcher TRPG.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.extendedCombat: Enables the optional combat overhaul (sword guard stances, etc.) on the action bar. Disable for RAW Witcher TRPG.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.farkleTable.Hi: Enables the Farkle gambling table — a GM-opened betting dice game (up to 4 seats, real dice items, currency stakes). Disable for RAW Witcher TRPG.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.dicePokerTable: Enables the Witcher dice poker table — a GM-opened betting dice game (up to 4 seats, real dice items, currency stakes). Disable for RAW Witcher TRPG.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.merchant.Name: Merchant
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.merchant.Hint: Enables the merchant actor: shops with dynamic pricing and buy/sell flow, plus scene merchant cards. Disable to remove merchant features.
When ON, movement can be split across your turn and interleaved with actions, up to your total SPD (e.g. SPD 8: move 3, act, move 2, extra action, move 3). When OFF (RAW), all movement must be taken at the start of your turn before any action — once you act, you forfeit any remaining movement.
WITCHER.Settings.Homebrew.rawToxicity.Hi: When ON (RAW, Core p.248), going over your toxicity cap inflicts the Overdosed condition (3 damage/turn, ignores armor) until your toxicity drops back to the cap or you pass a DC 18 Endurance check (1 action), which purges the last potion you drank. When OFF, toxicity is still tracked but never harms you — leave it off for a house rule.
WITCHER.Settings.CriticalWoundsPack.Name: Critical Wounds Compendium
WITCHER.Settings.CriticalWoundsPack.Hint: The Item compendium the critical-wound roller draws from. Wounds are matched by location, severity, and lesser/greater variant, so any compendium with critical-wound items works.
WOU.Parchment.EmptyBody: (No content yet — open the note to add a description.)
WOU.Parchment.SwipeTooltip: Take a copy into your character's inventory
WOU.Parchment.ConfirmRemove: Remove this parchment from the scene?
WOU.Parchment.NoGMOnline: Cannot post parchment — no GM is online to handle the request.
WOU.Parchment.NoOwnedCharacter: You don't own a character to swipe this into.
WOU.Parchment.ChooseCharacter: Choose which character takes the parchment:
WOU.Settings.Enabled.Name: Enable Chrome (Overhaul UI)
WOU.Settings.Enabled.Hint: Master switch for the overhaul-ui chrome bundled in this system. Toggling reloads Foundry.
WOU.Settings.TopChrome.Name: Top Chrome
WOU.Settings.TopChrome.Hint: Scene navigation strip + HUD numbers + notifications.
WOU.Settings.Sidebar.Name: Right Sidebar
WOU.Settings.SceneControls.Name: Left Scene Controls
WOU.Settings.SceneControls.Hint: Toolbar with token / measure / wall / lighting modes.
WOU.Settings.Hotbar.Name: Hotbar (Macro Belt)
WOU.Settings.Hotbar.Hint: Bottom macro slots — rearranged into a Witcher belt strip.
WOU.Settings.ActorSheets.Name: Actor Sheets
WOU.Settings.ActorSheets.Hint: Character / Monster / Loot / Mystery / Merchant sheet restyle.
WOU.Settings.ItemSheets.Name: Item Sheets
WOU.Settings.ItemSheets.Hint: All Witcher item types (weapon, spell, alchemy, etc.).
WOU.Settings.Chat.Name: Chat Cards
WOU.Settings.Chat.Hint: Typed chat messages (attack, defense, damage) and roll cards.
WOU.Settings.Compendium.Name: Compendium & Journals
WOU.Settings.MaxJournalEntries.Name: Max Journal Entries per Player
WOU.Settings.MaxJournalEntries.Hint: Cap how many journal entries each non-GM player may create. Set to 0 to disable the cap (default). GMs are unrestricted.
WOU.party.label: Party
WITCHER.Calendar.Name: Calendar of the Continent
WITCHER.Calendar.Description: The reckoning of days, months and seasons used across the Northern Kingdoms.
WITCHER.Calendar.Moon.Name: The Moon
WITCHER.Weather.CalYearHint: Type a year and press Enter to jump
WITCHER.Weather.GoToDay: Set game time to this day
WITCHER.Weather.RegenMonth: Regenerate this month's weather (a fresh draw; a slight change at the month edges)
WITCHER.Weather.ResetMonth: Reset this month to the default weather
WITCHER.Weather.ResetDay: Reset this day to the default weather
WITCHER.Weather.SceneMode.Hint: How this scene shows the world weather. Outdoors shows it in full; Indoors keeps a faded, muffled ambience but hides rain, tints and lightning; Disable Weather removes all weather sound and effects here.
WITCHER.Weather.SceneMode.NoTimeDarkness: Don't apply the day/night darkness cycle to this scene — its own authored lighting/darkness stays fixed regardless of the in-world time.
WITCHER.Weather.SceneMode.PanelNoScene: No scene is currently in view.
WITCHER.Weather.Map.ClearConfirm: Remove all painted terrain and the party marker from this scene? This cannot be undone.
WITCHER.Weather.Map.NoGrid: The weather map needs a gridded scene to paint on. This scene is gridless.
WITCHER.Weather.Map.ActiveMarkerCell: At the party marker on the map
WITCHER.Weather.Map.ActiveGlobal: Global weather — no world map designated
WITCHER.Weather.Map.ActiveGlobalNoMarker: Global weather — “{scene}” has no party marker
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Title: Weather & Calendar Configuration
WITCHER.Weather.Config.MenuHint: Every weather & time option in one place: the on/off toggles, automation, climate and clock speed, plus biomes, seasons, terrain landsites, condition penalties, and daylight/moon — all through friendly forms. Defaults are original to this system; you can edit, add, or remove anything.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.GeneralIntro: The main weather & time switches, gathered here instead of cluttering Foundry's settings list. The first sections are GM-only; Display knobs are per-player.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ResetConfirm: Discard your changes on this tab and restore the original defaults?
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ClimateIntro: Biomes set a region's year-round weather character; seasons shift it across the year. Bias sliders nudge how wet, windy, or cloudy that biome or season tends to be (negative = drier/calmer/clearer).
WITCHER.Weather.Config.AuroraHint: Allow auroras on clear, cold nights this season.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.TerrainIntro: Landsites are the terrain types you'll paint onto the weather map. Each layers its own local weather tweaks on top of the biome and season — mountains are colder and wetter, river valleys hold fog, coasts stay mild.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.WorldMapIntro: Designate the scene you've painted with terrain. The party marker on that scene sets the weather the party currently experiences; leave it unset to use one global climate everywhere.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.MapSceneHint: The scene whose painted terrain and party marker drive multi-region weather.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.LatitudeSpanHint: How much colder the map's north edge is than its south, edge to edge. 0 turns the latitude gradient off.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.FrontSweepDaysHin: Hours a weather front takes to cross the whole map, west to east. A small region (~180 km) is roughly 5 h; a continent is days (24 h = 1 day). Larger = slower, broader fronts.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ModifiersIntro: Each weather condition can apply mechanical penalties. Add a row per effect: pick what it affects, the modifier value (negative = penalty), the minimum intensity it kicks in at (1–3), and whether it scales with intensity.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.CalendarIntro: Daylight hours drive automatic scene darkness and the dawn/dusk jump buttons. The moon cycle sets how many days the moon takes to return to the same phase.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.MoonNote: Phase names and the eight-phase split are seeded from the Witcher calendar; only the total cycle length is adjustable here.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.RegionIntro: Where on the Continent is your campaign set? This slides every biome warmer or colder to match the latitude — the frozen north (Kovir) through to the savanna south (Ofir). Pick a region, or set your own offset.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.tempBase: The year-round average for this biome.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.tempSwing: How much hotter summers and colder winters get.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.dailySwing: How much colder it gets overnight.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.temp: Added on top of the biome's average for this season.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.tempOffset: Added on top of the surrounding region's temperature.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.elevation: Higher ground is colder; this helps work that out.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.swingMult: How much harsher (or milder) the temperature feels here.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.seasonMult: How different summer and winter are. Maritime coasts barely change; continental interiors swing hard.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.Hint.icon: A Font Awesome icon name, e.g. fa-solid fa-mountain.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ModMinLevelHint: How bad the weather must get before this kicks in.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ModPerLevelHint: Tick to make this grow stronger as the weather worsens.
WITCHER.Weather.Config.ModLabelPlacehold: e.g. Hard to see in the fog
WITCHER.Weather.Mod.SnowTrackFresh: Fresh tracks show plainly in snow
WITCHER.Weather.Mod.DarknessCloudSight: Night, cloud hides the moon — darkness
WITCHER.Weather.Mod.GlareSun: Daytime, clear desert sky — sun glare
WITCHER.Weather.Mod.GlareSnow: Daytime, clear sky — sun glaring off snow
WITCHER.Weather.Note.Heat: Extreme heat — Stamina is reduced by a third (halved in medium or heavy armor).
WITCHER.Weather.Note.GlareCombat: Glare — −3 to Attack and Defense when facing the sun.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.GrabHint: Take a condition when you are exposed to the weather without appropriate clothing or shelter. Applies to your selected tokens, or your own character; click again to remove it.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.NoTarget: No character to apply the condition to — select a token or assign a character first.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.HeatExhaustion: Take this when traveling or fighting in extreme heat (a desert or a heatwave). While active, your maximum Stamina is cut by a third — or in half if you are wearing medium or heavy armor.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.ChillyDesc: Take this when out in the cold without appropriate clothing. While active: −1 DEX.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.ColdDesc: Take this when exposed to the cold without appropriate clothing. While active: −1 DEX and −1 REF. Lightly clothed or naked, you can last about {hours} hours before the cold turns life-threatening.
WITCHER.Weather.Condition.FreezingDesc: Take this when exposed to freezing cold without appropriate clothing. While active: −2 DEX and −1 REF. Lightly clothed or naked, you can last about {hours} hours before the cold turns life-threatening.
WITCHER.Settings.SceneDefaults.MenuHint: Opens a hidden template scene in the normal scene configuration. Whatever you set here (token vision, lighting, grid, environment…) is copied onto every new blank scene you create. It is a default, not a global override — existing scenes are never changed.
WITCHER.Settings.SceneDefaults.Created: Created the Default Scene Template. Adjust its settings and close — new scenes will inherit them.
WITCHER.Settings.AdrenalineEnabled.Name: Adrenaline (optional rule)
WITCHER.Settings.AdrenalineEnabled.Hint: Use the Core optional Adrenaline rule (p.175-176): bank a d6 per critical hit (pool capped at BODY), then spend dice for +1d6 damage or +1d6 temporary HP, paying Stamina per die. Turn this OFF to remove adrenaline from character sheets, the chrome UI, the combat dock, and weapon attack dialogs entirely. Requires reload.
WITCHER.Settings.AdrenalineStaPerDie.Hin: Stamina spent for each adrenaline die committed to damage or temp HP. Core RAW is 10 (p.176). Only used when the Adrenaline optional rule is on.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherEnabled.Name: Inbuilt time & weather
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherEnabled.Hint: Show the system's own date/time/weather widget and calendar. Turn this OFF to use an external calendar or weather module instead — combat round timers, oil/coating countdowns, and status-effect durations keep working either way, since they run on Foundry's world time. Requires reload.
WITCHER.Settings.TimeFlowRate.Name: Clock speed (running time)
WITCHER.Settings.TimeFlowRate.Hint: In-world seconds that pass per real second while the game is unpaused, so the clock runs on its own. 1 = real time (the default). Raise it for a faster narrative clock — e.g. 60 = one in-world minute per real second (a full day in 24 real minutes). Set to 0 to freeze the clock and move time only by hand or in combat. The GM drives it and it stays synced for everyone; pausing stops it.
WITCHER.Settings.AutoSceneDarkness.Name: Auto scene darkness
WITCHER.Settings.AutoSceneDarkness.Hint: Let the inbuilt clock drive the viewed scene's darkness — bright at midday, dark at night, ramping across dawn and dusk, with storms and fog dimming the daytime. GM only; ignored when 'Inbuilt time & weather' is off.
WITCHER.Settings.AutoWeatherFx.Name: Auto weather effects
WITCHER.Settings.AutoWeatherFx.Hint: Draw the current weather (rain, snow, hail, sandstorm, fog, clouds, lightning) on the scene canvas. GM only; ignored when 'Inbuilt time & weather' is off.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherSound.Name: Weather ambience
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherSound.Hint: Let the GM drive a 'Weather' playlist whose looping stems (rain, hail, wind, blizzard) follow the current weather. Plays for everyone — adjust loudness with the Environment volume slider in the Playlists sidebar.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherDebug.Name: Weather debug badge
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherDebug.Hint: Show a numeric intensity badge (e.g. "[cloud 2 · snow 1 · wind 0]") next to weather labels in the Time & Weather console, so you can see which tier each layer is running at. GM aid; per-client; never shown to players.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherThunder.Name: Thunder
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherThunder.Hint: Play thunderclaps during lightning storms. Per-client; mute it here without affecting other players.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherMaxParticles.Hin: Maximum number of weather particles drawn at once. Lower this if weather effects stutter on your machine. Per-client.
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherBiome.Name: Weather climate
WITCHER.Settings.WeatherBiome.Hint: The regional climate that drives the inbuilt weather engine. Temperate is the default Continent lowland; Highland is colder and windier; Coastal is wet and stormy; Arid is hot and dry.
WITCHER.Farkle.title: Farkle
WITCHER.Die.faceImageHint: Click to set the texture for this face (empty = default).
WITCHER.Die.weightHint: Relative landing weight for this face (1 = fair, 0 = never).
WITCHER.Farkle.board.rollHint: Drag back from the dice and release to throw.
WITCHER.Farkle.board.selectHint: Click the scoring dice on the table, then Set Aside.
WITCHER.Farkle.board.decideHint: Bank your points, or roll the dice still in hand.
WITCHER.Farkle.warn.invalidSelection: Those dice don't all score. Pick only scoring dice.
WITCHER.Farkle.invite.title: Farkle Invitation
WITCHER.Farkle.invite.body: {name} challenges you to a game of Farkle. Sit down?
WITCHER.Farkle.invite.waiting: Waiting for your opponent to take a seat…
WITCHER.Farkle.rules.title: Farkle — Rules
WITCHER.Farkle.rules.flow: Both players ante into the pot. On your turn, throw six dice and set aside at least one scoring die, then choose to bank your turn total or roll the dice that remain. Roll no scoring dice and you Farkle — your turn total is lost. First to {target} points banks the win and takes the pot.
WITCHER.Farkle.rules.hotDice: Hot dice: set aside all six scoring dice and you keep your total, then roll a fresh six to press your luck.
WITCHER.Farkle.rules.farkle: Farkle: a throw with no scoring dice ends your turn and forfeits everything you'd set aside that turn.

WITCHER.Farkle.log.newMatch: A new game begins. Ante {ante} — first to {target} takes the pot.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.youFarkle: Farkle! You score nothing this turn.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.hotDice: Hot dice! A fresh six to roll.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.npcBanked: Your opponent banks {pts} — their score is {total}.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.npcFarkle: Your opponent Farkles and scores nothing.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.oppBanked: Your opponent banks — their score is {total}.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.oppFarkle: Your opponent Farkles and scores nothing.
WITCHER.Farkle.log.youLose: Your opponent takes the pot — {pot} crowns.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.title: Farkle Table
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.diceHint: Pick up to {n} dice. Extras are left in the bag; with fewer, the rest are house dice.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.noDice: This actor has no Die items — house dice will be used.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.cancelled: The GM ended the Farkle table; your ante was refunded.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.noCharacter: Assign a character to your user to join the table.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.cantAfford: That actor cannot cover the ante.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.needTwo: At least two seats must be filled to start.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.kicked: {name} can no longer cover the ante and was removed.
WITCHER.Farkle.lobby.disabled: The Farkle Table homebrew rule is disabled.
WITCHER.DicePoker.title: Dice Poker
WITCHER.DicePoker.board.betHint: Place your bet: check, raise, call — or surrender the hand.
WITCHER.DicePoker.board.rollHint: Drag back from the dice and release to throw all five.
WITCHER.DicePoker.board.selectHint: Tap the dice on your rim you want to REROLL (they glow gold). Then press Reroll to throw just those — or Stand to keep all five.
WITCHER.DicePoker.rules.title: Dice Poker — Rules
WITCHER.DicePoker.rules.flow: Heads-up, it's best of three hands. Both players ante, then throw their opening five. A betting round follows — check, raise, call, or surrender. Survivors then keep any dice they like and reroll the rest once; the better final hand takes the pot and scores the hand. First to two hands wins the match. (With three or more players it's a single hand, no betting — best hand takes the whole pot.)
WITCHER.DicePoker.rules.tie: An exact tie for the best hand replays the round, with the pot carried over.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.newMatch: A new hand begins. Ante {ante} — best hand takes the pot.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.youKept: You keep {n} and reroll the rest.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.npcKept: Your opponent keeps {n} and rerolls the rest.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.npcForfeit: {name} is forced to forfeit and leaves the table.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.foldWin: {name} folded — you take the pot of {pot} uncontested.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.foldLose: You folded — the pot of {pot} goes to your opponent.
WITCHER.DicePoker.log.foldWinner: Everyone folded to {name}, who takes the pot of {pot}.
WITCHER.DicePoker.chat.credit: {name} leaves the table owing {owed} on credit — their losses ran deeper than their purse.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.title: Dice Poker Table
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.diceHint: Pick up to {n} dice. Extras are left in the bag; with fewer, the rest are house dice.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.noDice: This actor has no Die items — house dice will be used.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.formatHint: How long the table runs. Heads-up: Best-of-N ends once a player wins the majority of hands. With three or more players, play a fixed number of hands (richest purse wins) instead. Continuous keeps dealing until someone can't cover the ante or quits.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.cancelled: The GM ended the Dice Poker table; your ante was refunded.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.noCharacter: Assign a character to your user to join the table.

WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.needTwo: At least two seats must be filled to start.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.kicked: {name} can no longer cover the ante and was removed.
WITCHER.DicePoker.lobby.disabled: The Dice Poker Table homebrew rule is disabled.
